message:
fix(mission-control): dropdown overlap, transparency, and silent AI errors (#5915)

Fixes three Mission Control bugs in a single change.

#5906 — Target cluster dropdown overlapped the Payload section and was
transparent. The dropdown panel used `bg-popover`, which is not a defined
Tailwind color in this codebase, so the class was a no-op and the panel
rendered with no background. The container also only had `relative z-20`,
which lost the stacking battle against the Payload Grid below (each
PayloadCard is a `motion.div` whose `transform` creates a new stacking
context). Bumped the container to `z-dropdown` (the semantic 100 layer)
and switched the panel background to `bg-card` so it is fully opaque.

#5907 — When the AI provider failed (auth error, rate limit, agent not
running, etc.), Mission Control's Suggest button silently spun and then
gave up. The underlying planning mission already transitioned to `failed`
and `useMissions` already attached a system message describing the error,
the same way the AI Missions sidebar does — Mission Control just never
rendered it. Added an `AISuggestErrorBanner` that watches the planning
mission status and surfaces the latest system message inline with a Retry
button, mirroring the sidebar's UX.

#5911 — "Suggest doesn't return results." This is the user-visible
symptom of #5907: the planning mission is failing on the AI provider call
but the UI shows nothing, so it looks like Suggest is broken. With the
banner from #5907 in place, the actual error (auth, rate limit, no agent,
etc.) is now visible and actionable, and the Retry button re-runs
`askAIForSuggestions` against the same planning mission. No second
endpoint or alternate code path is involved — Mission Control and the AI
Missions sidebar share the same `useMissions` context and `startMission`
flow.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@ import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from 'react' import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion' -import { Sparkles, Loader2, Plus, Search, Info, Shield, Eye, Network, Box, Lock, Layers, Server, X as XIcon } from 'lucide-react' +import { Sparkles, Loader2, Plus, Search, Info, Shield, Eye, Network, Box, Lock, Layers, Server, X as XIcon, AlertTriangle, RotateCcw } from 'lucide-react' import { Button } from '../ui/Button' import { PayloadGrid } from './PayloadGrid' import type { MissionControlState, PayloadProject } from './types' @@ -98,6 +98,18 @@ export function FixerDefinitionPanel({ .filter((m) => m.role === 'assistant') .slice(-1)[0] + // Surface AI provider failures (#5907 / #5911). When the planning mission + // transitions to 'failed', useMissions appends a system message describing + // the underlying error (auth failure, rate limit, agent unavailable, etc.). + // Mission Control previously rendered nothing in this state, leaving users + // staring at a stalled UI. Mirror the AI Missions sidebar pattern: display + // the latest system message inline so the user knows what went wrong and + // how to recover. + const planningFailed = planningMission?.status === 'failed' + const latestSystemError = planningFailed + ? planningMission?.messages.filter((m) => m.role === 'system').slice(-1)[0] + : undefined + // Summary stats for left sidebar const categoryCounts = (() => { const counts: Record = {} @@ -264,6 +276,15 @@ export function FixerDefinitionPanel({ )} + {/* AI provider failure banner (#5907 / #5911) */} + {planningFailed && !aiStreaming && ( + onAskAI(state.description, state.projects)} + disabled={!state.description.trim()} + /> + )} + {/* AI Executive Analysis */} {latestAIMessage && !aiStreaming && ( void + disabled: boolean +}) { + // Fall back to a generic message when the failed mission has no system + // message attached (e.g. silent WebSocket failure). + const message = errorContent.trim() || + 'AI Suggest failed. The AI provider returned an error or the agent is not reachable. ' + + 'Check your provider configuration in Settings, make sure your local agent is running, and try again.' + + return ( + +
